title: Taste constraint rules with per-pack profiles and severity config
short_description: Ship PACING.BEAT_STALL, PACING.ENTRANCE_STORM, PACING.SCENE_DENSITY, CONTINUITY.COLOR_DRIFT, and CONTINUITY.CAMERA_TELEPORT with per-pack constraint-profile thresholds (crude tolerates what flat forbids, tested with fixtures in both packs) plus per-project severity promote/demote visible in report metadata.
long_description_md:
# Taste constraint rules with per-pack profiles and severity config

## Why this task exists

Verification without taste rules only catches wrongness, not badness: a scene graph with no overlaps and correct references can still stall for ten seconds on nothing, dump fifteen characters onstage at once, or jump-cut the map camera across the globe with no transition. Capability 7 of the product spec and the Phase 5 done-when list name five specific rules for exactly this gap: `PACING.BEAT_STALL`, `PACING.ENTRANCE_STORM`, `PACING.SCENE_DENSITY`, `CONTINUITY.COLOR_DRIFT`, `CONTINUITY.CAMERA_TELEPORT`. These are where anima-ai's "taste encoded as constraint rules" design principle (COFOUNDER_PERSONA.md) gets tested for real: pacing and continuity are exactly the kind of quality property a lesser tool would bolt on a VLM check for. Here it has to be fully deterministic, fully pack-aware, and fully explainable via `fix_hint`.

## Roadmap fit

Lands in Phase 5, after both launch packs exist (`crude-pack`, `flat-pack`) and after map camera flights ship (`map-camera-state`), because `CONTINUITY.CAMERA_TELEPORT` needs real camera-path data to reason about, and every rule needs real fixture content in both packs to prove per-pack tuning is genuine rather than a single hardcoded threshold. It completes capability 7 alongside the structural and layout/readability rules already shipped in Phase 2 (`verify-structural-checks`, `verification-report-schema`).

## Required contract and scope

Each of the five rules is a versioned, registered Finding-producing rule per the `verification-report-schema` contract (namespaced `code`, restricted `severity`, `beat_ids`/`node_ids`, optional `t_window`, `expected`/`actual`, required non-empty `fix_hint`, `rule_version`). None may duplicate an already-registered code; all run inside the normal `anima verify` pipeline.

- **`PACING.BEAT_STALL`**: flags a beat (or contiguous span) where on-screen content is static beyond a pack-defined maximum idle duration with no entrance/exit/transform/camera movement.
- **`PACING.ENTRANCE_STORM`**: flags too many simultaneous or near-simultaneous entrance animations within a pack-defined window, above a pack-defined count threshold.
- **`PACING.SCENE_DENSITY`**: flags a scene whose concurrent on-screen node count exceeds a pack-defined ceiling at any instant.
- **`CONTINUITY.COLOR_DRIFT`**: tracks a character/prop/territory's resolved palette-role usage across scenes and flags an unexplained/unintentional shift outside pack-defined tolerance between appearances.
- **`CONTINUITY.CAMERA_TELEPORT`**: using the map camera state/flight data from `map-camera-state`, flags a cross-scene camera jump (center/zoom delta) exceeding a pack-defined maximum without an intervening flight/transition.

**Per-pack constraint profiles are the core of this task, not an afterthought.** Every threshold above (idle duration, simultaneous-entrance count/window, density ceiling, color-drift tolerance, camera-jump tolerance) must be sourced from each `StylePack`'s constraint profile (the schema `style-pack-system` already defines), and `crude`'s profile must be measurably looser than `flat`'s on at least one axis per rule ‚Äî this is the literal Phase-5 requirement ("crude tolerates what flat forbids, tested"). Prove it with shared borderline fixtures: a fixture engineered to sit between the two packs' thresholds must pass verify under `crude` and fail under `flat` (or the reverse, wherever the pack's aesthetic genuinely calls for the opposite direction ‚Äî e.g. flat's calm/geometric brief may tolerate longer stalls than crude's comedic pace-driven brief; pick and document the correct direction per rule rather than assuming one direction for all five).

**Per-project severity override.** Projects may promote or demote any of these five rule codes' severity (e.g. downgrade `PACING.SCENE_DENSITY` from error to warning for a deliberately busy project, or promote a warning to error for a stricter pipeline). Overrides must be validated against the existing code registry (reject overrides for unregistered codes), must flow into the actual `Finding.severity` emitted (and therefore into `pass`/render-gate behavior per `verification-report-schema`'s render-gate policy), and must be visible in the report's metadata (which codes were overridden, from what default to what value) so an agent or human reading a report can see the override took effect rather than silently guessing.

## Out of scope

- Building or extending crude or flat pack assets, palettes, or manifests ‚Äî owned by `crude-pack` and `flat-pack`.
- The full zero-code-diff pack-pluggability proof (structural graph diffing, vocabulary parity across packs) ‚Äî owned by `pack-pluggability-proof`. This task's fixtures only need to exist in both packs to prove threshold tuning; they are not required to be the same shared fixture used for the pluggability proof.
- Structural, layout/readability, or palette rules (`REF.*`, `OVERLAP.*`, `MARGIN.*`, `FONT.MIN_SIZE`, `TIMING.READ_TIME`, `CONTRAST.LOW`, `STYLE.PALETTE_VIOLATION`) ‚Äî already shipped in Phase 2/`style-pack-system`.
- Map-specific structural rules (`MAP.UNKNOWN_TERRITORY`, `MAP.ARROW_OUT_OF_VIEW`, `MAP.DATE_MISMATCH`) ‚Äî Phase 4 scope, not this task.
- The founder style checkpoint judgment itself ‚Äî that stays in `flat-pack`.
- Global (non-taste) severity/config plumbing beyond these five codes, or a general project-config schema redesign.

## Known overlap flagged for the orchestrator

`flat-pack.json`'s third deliverable already claims to implement these exact five rules, per-pack profiles, severity overrides, and a pack-parity test in one bundled deliverable (`tests/verification/test_taste_constraints.py`, `tests/verification/test_continuity_constraints.py`, `tests/integration/test_taste_constraint_profiles.py`, `tests/integration/test_rule_severity_overrides.py`, `tests/integration/test_style_pack_parity.py`). That is direct duplication with both this task and `pack-pluggability-proof`. This task is the authoritative, deeper implementation (borderline-fixture proof of per-pack direction, code-registry-validated overrides, report-metadata visibility); whoever executes `flat-pack` should treat its own taste-rule deliverable as satisfied by this task rather than re-implementing a thinner version. Left as-is here since spec files are not being retroactively edited as part of this pass.

deliverables.description: Implement and register the three pacing rules (`PACING.BEAT_STALL`, `PACING.ENTRANCE_STORM`, `PACING.SCENE_DENSITY`) as versioned Finding-producing rules reading thresholds from each pack's constraint profile, with violating and passing fixtures in both crude and flat proving each rule fires and each rule stays silent on clean content.

Implement and register the two continuity rules (`CONTINUITY.COLOR_DRIFT`, `CONTINUITY.CAMERA_TELEPORT`) as versioned Finding-producing rules tracking cross-scene palette-role usage and camera center/zoom deltas from the `map-camera-state` camera-flight data, with violating and passing fixtures in both crude and flat.

Per-pack profile differential test: for each of the five rules, a shared borderline fixture engineered to sit between the crude and flat thresholds must pass verify under one pack's profile and fail under the other, proving the two packs' constraint profiles are genuinely different thresholds and not a single hardcoded value read twice.

Per-project severity promote/demote for the five taste/continuity codes: validated against the existing code registry (reject overrides for unregistered codes), flows into emitted Finding.severity and therefore render-gate pass/fail, and is recorded in the VerificationReport metadata (code, default severity, overridden severity) visible in both `anima verify --json` and human output.

verification.command: pytest -q tests/integration/test_taste_rule_severity_overrides.py && ruff check src/anima/verification/taste tests/verification/test_pacing_rules.py tests/verification/test_continuity_rules.py && mypy --strict src/anima/verification/taste
